Now I have a good picture of what data is available. Let me also look at some commonly used project dashboards to think about what to include. Let me fetch some inspiration.

Actually, let me think about this from first principles. The key thing about this analytics dashboard is that it's for opencode usage. It should tell a story about how the tool is being used.

**Data available and chart ideas:**

1. **Time series: Sessions per day** — bar chart showing count of sessions started each day
2. **Time series: Messages per day** — bar chart showing message volume over time  
3. **Hourly activity heatmap** — messages by hour of day (shows when the user is most active)
4. **Token usage breakdown** — stacked bar showing input vs output vs reasoning tokens per session/model
5. **Top sessions by token usage** — horizontal bar chart 
6. **Model distribution** — bar/table of models used
7. **Agent distribution** — pie/bar of agent types
8. **Session duration** — bar chart of session lengths
9. **Cumulative messages over time** — line chart showing growth
10. **Tool usage treemap or bar** — already have tool leaderboard but could visualize
11. **Daily active sessions** — how many unique sessions per day

- A **charts row** at the top with 2-3 Chart.js charts
- A **time-series section** below

bashcompletedGet comprehensive chart data▶
Input
{
  "command": "python3 -c \"\nimport sqlite3\nconn = sqlite3.connect('/root/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db')\ncur = conn.cursor()\n\n# Sessions per day\ncur.execute(\\\"SELECT date(time_created/1000,'unixepoch') as d,COUNT(*) FROM session GROUP BY d ORDER BY d\\\")\nspd = cur.fetchall()\nprint('sessions_per_day:', spd)\n\n# Messages per day\ncur.execute(\\\"SELECT date(time_created/1000,'unixepoch') as d,COUNT(*) FROM message GROUP BY d ORDER BY d\\\")\nmpd = cur.fetchall()\nprint('messages_per_day:', mpd)\n\n# Messages by hour\ncur.execute(\\\"SELECT CAST(strftime('%H',time_created/1000,'unixepoch') AS INTEGER) as h,COUNT(*) FROM message GROUP BY h ORDER BY h\\\")\nmbh = cur.fetchall()\nprint('messages_by_hour:', mbh)\n\n# Tokens per model\ncur.execute(\\\"SELECT json_extract(data,'$.modelID'),SUM(tokens_input),SUM(tokens_output),SUM(tokens_reasoning) FROM session CROSS JOIN message ON message.session_id=session.id WHERE json_extract(data,'$.role')='assistant' AND json_extract(data,'$.modelID') IS NOT NULL GROUP BY json_extract(data,'$.modelID') ORDER BY SUM(tokens_input)+SUM(tokens_output) DESC\\\")\ntpm = cur.fetchall()\nprint('tokens_per_model:', tpm)\n\n# Top 15 sessions by total tokens\ncur.execute('SELECT slug,model,tokens_input+tokens_output+tokens_reasoning as t FROM session WHERE slug IS NOT NULL ORDER BY t DESC LIMIT 15')\ntps = cur.fetchall()\nprint('top_sessions_tokens:', tps)\n\n# Session duration buckets\ncur.execute('SELECT CAST(ROUND((time_updated-time_created)/300000.0)*5 AS INTEGER) as bucket,COUNT(*) FROM session WHERE time_updated>time_created GROUP BY bucket ORDER BY bucket')\ndura = cur.fetchall()\nprint('duration_buckets(min):', dura)\n\n# Tool calls per day\ncur.execute(\\\"SELECT date(p.time_created/1000,'unixepoch') as d,COUNT(*) FROM part p WHERE json_extract(p.data,'$.type')='tool' GROUP BY d ORDER BY d\\\")\ntpd = cur.fetchall()\nprint('tools_per_day:', tpd)\n\"",
  "description": "Get comprehensive chart data"
}

Input
{
  "filePath": "/tmp/opencode_analytics_v7.py",
  "oldString": "def generate_stats(sessions,tool_occ,model_occ,prov_occ):\n    providers=Counter(); models=Counter(); tools_used=Counter(); tm=0; tt=0; tl=0\n    for s in sessions:\n        providers[s['provider'] or 'Unknown']+=1\n        tm+=s['messages']; tt+=s['tool_calls']; tl+=s['llm_calls']\n    # Use DB occurrence counts for tools, models, providers\n    for t,c in tool_occ.items(): tools_used[t]=c\n    for m,c in model_occ.items(): models[m]=c\n    for p,c in prov_occ.items():\n        if p not in providers: providers[p]=c\n    return {'total_sessions':len(sessions),'total_messages':tm,'total_tool_calls':tt,'total_llm_calls':tl,'providers':providers,'models':models,'tools_used':tools_used}",
  "newString": "def generate_stats(sessions,tool_occ,model_occ,prov_occ):\n    providers=Counter(); models=Counter(); tools_used=Counter(); tm=0; tt=0; tl=0\n    for s in sessions:\n        providers[s['provider'] or 'Unknown']+=1\n        tm+=s['messages']; tt+=s['tool_calls']; tl+=s['llm_calls']\n    for t,c in tool_occ.items(): tools_used[t]=c\n    for m,c in model_occ.items(): models[m]=c\n    for p,c in prov_occ.items():\n        if p not in providers: providers[p]=c\n    return {'total_sessions':len(sessions),'total_messages':tm,'total_tool_calls':tt,'total_llm_calls':tl,'providers':providers,'models':models,'tools_used':tools_used}\n\ndef get_chart_data():\n    data={'days':{},'hours':[],'model_tokens':[],'top_sessions':[],'tools_daily':{}}\n    try:\n        conn=sqlite3.connect(str(DB_PATH)); cur=conn.cursor()\n        cur.execute(\"SELECT date(time_created/1000,'unixepoch') as d,COUNT(*) FROM message GROUP BY d ORDER BY d\")\n        for d,c in cur.fetchall(): data['days'][d]=[c,0,0]\n        cur.execute(\"SELECT date(time_created/1000,'unixepoch') as d,COUNT(*) FROM session GROUP BY d ORDER BY d\")\n        for d,c in cur.fetchall():\n            if d in data['days']: data['days'][d][1]=c\n            else: data['days'][d]=[0,c,0]\n        cur.execute(\"SELECT date(p.time_created/1000,'unixepoch') as d,COUNT(*) FROM part p WHERE json_extract(p.data,'$.type')='tool' GROUP BY d ORDER BY d\")\n        for d,c in cur.fetchall():\n            if d in data['days']: data['days'][d][2]=c\n            else: data['days'][d]=[0,0,c]\n        cur.execute(\"SELECT CAST(strftime('%H',time_created/1000,'unixepoch') AS INTEGER),COUNT(*) FROM message GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1\")\n        data['hours']=[(r[0],r[1]) for r in cur.fetchall()]\n        cur.execute(\"SELECT json_extract(s.data,'$.modelID'),SUM(s.tokens_input),SUM(s.tokens_output),SUM(s.tokens_reasoning) FROM message s JOIN message ON 1=1 WHERE 1=0 GROUP BY 1\")\n        cur.execute(\"SELECT json_extract(data,'$.modelID'),SUM(tokens_input),SUM(tokens_output),SUM(tokens_reasoning) FROM message WHERE json_extract(data,'$.role')='assistant' AND json_extract(data,'$.modelID') IS NOT NULL AND tokens_input>0 GROUP BY json_extract(data,'$.modelID') ORDER BY SUM(tokens_input)+SUM(tokens_output) DESC\")\n        data['model_tokens']=[(r[0],r[1],r[2],r[3]) for r in cur.fetchall()]\n        cur.execute(\"SELECT slug,json_extract(model,'$.id'),tokens_input+tokens_output+tokens_reasoning FROM session WHERE slug IS NOT NULL ORDER BY tokens_input+tokens_output+tokens_reasoning DESC LIMIT 15\")\n        data['top_sessions']=[(r[0] or '?',r[1] or '?',r[2]) for r in cur.fetchall()]\n        conn.close()\n    except: pass\n    return data"
}

Wait, I have a dummy invalid join in the model_tokens query. Let me fix that. The tokens_input/tokens_output/tokens_reasoning are in the `message` table columns, not in the JSON data. Let me check the message table columns again.

From earlier:
```
--- message columns ---
(0, 'id', 'TEXT')
(1, 'session_id', 'TEXT')
(2, 'time_created', 'INTEGER')
(3, 'time_updated', 'INTEGER')
(4, 'data', 'TEXT')
```

So tokens are NOT columns in message table - they're in the session table. Let me fix the query. I need to join message with session and sum the token columns from session... but that would duplicate tokens across messages. 

Actually, tokens_input/tokens_output/tokens_reasoning are columns in the **session** table, not the message table. They're per-session cumulative values. So to get tokens per model, I can query the session table directly.

editcompletedtmp/opencode_analytics_v7.py▶
Input
{
  "filePath": "/tmp/opencode_analytics_v7.py",
  "newString": "def gen_index(stats, sessions, chart_data):\n    models_total=sum(stats['models'].values())\n    model_rows=\"\\n\".join(\n        f'<div class=\"model-row\"><span class=\"model-name\">{html.escape(m)}</span><div class=\"model-bar\"><div class=\"model-bar-fill\" style=\"width:{c/models_total*100:.1f}%\"></div></div><span class=\"model-count\">{c}</span></div>'\n        for m,c in stats['models'].most_common())\n\n    tool_items=\"\"\n    for i,(t,c) in enumerate(stats['tools_used'].most_common(15)):\n        tool_items+=f'<div class=\"tool-rank\"><span class=\"tool-rank-num\">#{i+1}</span><span class=\"tool-rank-name\">{html.escape(t)}</span><span class=\"tool-rank-count\">{c}</span></div>'\n\n    prov_items=\"\"\n    color_idx=0\n    for p,c in stats['providers'].most_common():\n        prov_items+=f'<span class=\"badge provider-{color_idx%2}\"><span class=\"name\">{html.escape(p)}</span><span class=\"count\">{c}</span></span>'\n        color_idx+=1\n\n    sessions_rows=\"\"\n    for i,s in enumerate(sessions):\n        label=session_label(s)\n        sessions_rows+=f'''<tr><td class=\"col-num\">{i+1}</td><td class=\"col-name\"><a href=\"session_{i+1}.html\">{label}</a></td><td class=\"col-provider\">{html.escape(s['provider'] or '?')}</td><td class=\"col-model\">{html.escape(s['model'] or '?')}</td><td class=\"col-msgs\">{s['messages']}</td><td class=\"col-tools\">{len(s['tools'])}</td><td class=\"col-calls\">{s['tool_calls']}</td><td class=\"col-view\"><a href=\"session_{i+1}.html\">open</a></td></tr>'''\n\n    # Build chart JSON data\n    days_sorted=sorted(chart_data['days'].items())\n    day_labels=json.dumps([d for d,_,_ in days_sorted])\n    day_messages=json.dumps([m for _,m,_ in days_sorted])\n    day_sessions=json.dumps([s for _,_,s in days_sorted])\n    hour_labels=json.dumps([f'{h:02d}:00' for h,_ in chart_data['hours']])\n    hour_values=json.dumps([c for _,c in chart_data['hours']])\n    mt_labels=json.dumps([m for m,_,_,_ in chart_data['model_tokens']])\n    mt_input=json.dumps([i for _,i,_,_ in chart_data['model_tokens']])\n    mt_output=json.dumps([o for _,_,o,_ in chart_data['model_tokens']])\n    mt_reasoning=json.dumps([r for _,_,_,r in chart_data['model_tokens']])\n    ts_labels=json.dumps([s for s,_,_ in chart_data['top_sessions']])\n    ts_tokens=json.dumps([t for _,_,t in chart_data['top_sessions']])\n\n    return f'''<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n<head><meta charset=\"UTF-8\"><meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1.0\"><title>Session Logs</title>\n<script src=\"https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@4\"></script>\n<style>{CSS}\n.charts-row {{ display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr); gap:12px; margin-bottom:16px; }}\n.chart-term {{ background:#131720; border:1px solid #2A3040; border-radius:8px; }}\n.chart-body {{ padding:10px; }}\n.chart-body canvas {{ width:100% !important; height:160px !important; }}\n@media(max-width:1100px){{.charts-row{{grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;}}}}\n@media(max-width:700px){{.charts-row{{grid-template-columns:1fr;}}}}\n</style></head>\n<body><div class=\"scanline\"></div>\n<div class=\"container\">\n\n<div class=\"top-bar\">\n<div class=\"top-bar-left\"><div class=\"top-bar-logo\">{LOGO}</div><span class=\"top-bar-title\">Session Logs</span><span class=\"top-bar-badge\">v1.0</span></div>\n<div class=\"top-bar-right\"><span class=\"top-bar-dot\"></span> live &middot; {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')}</div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"stats-grid\">\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{stats['total_sessions']}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Sessions</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{stats['total_messages']}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Messages</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{stats['total_llm_calls']}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">LLM Calls</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{stats['total_tool_calls']}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Tool Calls</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{len(stats['providers'])}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Providers</div></div>\n<div class=\"status-left\"><span class=\"status-item\"><span class=\"status-dot cyan\"></span> {len(sessions)} sessions</span><span class=\"status-item\"><span class=\"status-dot green\"></span> {stats['total_messages']} messages</span><span class=\"status-item\"><span class=\"status-dot purple\"></span> {stats['total_tool_calls']} tool calls</span></div>\n<div class=\"status-right\"><span class=\"status-item\">opencode analytics</span><span class=\"status-item\">generated {datetime.now().strftime('%H:%M')}</span></div>\n</div>\n</div>\n<script>\nChart.defaults.color=\"#6A7894\"; Chart.defaults.font.family=\"'Inter',sans-serif\";\nChart.register({id:\"transparent\",beforeDraw:function(c){var ctx=c.ctx;ctx.save();ctx.clearRect(0,0,c.width,c.height);ctx.restore()}});\nvar dly={ld:{!!day_labels!!},m:{!!day_messages!!},s:{!!day_sessions!!}};\nnew Chart(document.getElementById(\"chartDaily\"),{type:\"bar\",data:{labels:dly.ld,datasets:[{label:\"messages\",data:dly.m,backgroundColor:\"#00D4FF66\",borderColor:\"#00D4FF\",borderWidth:1,yAxisID:\"y\"},{label:\"sessions\",data:dly.s,type:\"line\",borderColor:\"#00FF88\",backgroundColor:\"#00FF8833\",pointStyle:\"circle\",pointRadius:3,yAxisID:\"y1\"}]},options:{responsive:true,maintainAspectRatio:false,plugins:{legend:{display:false}},scales:{x:{display:true,grid:{color:\"#1A1F2B\"},ticks:{font:{size:10}}},y:{display:false,grid:{color:\"#1A1F2B\"},beginAtZero:true},y1:{display:false,position:\"right\",grid:{drawOnChartArea:false},beginAtZero:true}}}});\nnew Chart(document.getElementById(\"chartHourly\"),{type:\"bar\",data:{labels:{!!hour_labels!!},datasets:[{label:\"messages\",data:{!!hour_values!!},backgroundColor:\"#B388FF66\",borderColor:\"#B388FF\",borderWidth:1}]},options:{responsive:true,maintainAspectRatio:false,plugins:{legend:{display:false}},scales:{x:{display:true,grid:{color:\"#1A1F2B\"},ticks:{font:{size:9}}},y:{display:false,grid:{color:\"#1A1F2B\"},beginAtZero:true}}}});\nnew Chart(document.getElementById(\"chartTokens\"),{type:\"bar\",data:{labels:{!!mt_labels!!},datasets:[{label:\"input\",data:{!!mt_input!!},backgroundColor:\"#00D4FF66\",borderColor:\"#00D4FF\",borderWidth:1},{label:\"output\",data:{!!mt_output!!},backgroundColor:\"#00FF8866\",borderColor:\"#00FF88\",borderWidth:1},{label:\"reasoning\",data:{!!mt_reasoning!!},backgroundColor:\"#FF8A5066\",borderColor:\"#FF8A50\",borderWidth:1}]},options:{responsive:true,maintainAspectRatio:false,plugins:{legend:{labels:{boxWidth:8,font:{size:10}}}},scales:{x:{display:true,grid:{color:\"#1A1F2B\"},ticks:{font:{size:9}}},y:{display:false,grid:{color:\"#1A1F2B\"},beginAtZero:true}}}});\n</script>\n</body></html>'''",
  "oldString": "def gen_index(stats, sessions, chart_data):\n    models_total=sum(stats['models'].values())\n    model_rows=\"\\n\".join(\n        f'<div class=\"model-row\"><span class=\"model-name\">{html.escape(m)}</span><div class=\"model-bar\"><div class=\"model-bar-fill\" style=\"width:{c/models_total*100:.1f}%\"></div></div><span class=\"model-count\">{c}</span></div>'\n        for m,c in stats['models'].most_common())\n\n    # Tool list for side panel\n    tool_items=\"\"\n    for i,(t,c) in enumerate(stats['tools_used'].most_common(15)):\n        tool_items+=f'<div class=\"tool-rank\"><span class=\"tool-rank-num\">#{i+1}</span><span class=\"tool-rank-name\">{html.escape(t)}</span><span class=\"tool-rank-count\">{c}</span></div>'\n\n    prov_items=\"\"\n    color_idx=0\n    for p,c in stats['providers'].most_common():\n        prov_items+=f'<span class=\"badge provider-{color_idx%2}\"><span class=\"name\">{html.escape(p)}</span><span class=\"count\">{c}</span></span>'\n        color_idx+=1\n\n    sessions_rows=\"\"\n    for i,s in enumerate(sessions):\n        label=session_label(s)\n        sessions_rows+=f'''<tr><td class=\"col-num\">{i+1}</td><td class=\"col-name\"><a href=\"session_{i+1}.html\">{label}</a></td><td class=\"col-provider\">{html.escape(s['provider'] or '?')}</td><td class=\"col-model\">{html.escape(s['model'] or '?')}</td><td class=\"col-msgs\">{s['messages']}</td><td class=\"col-tools\">{len(s['tools'])}</td><td class=\"col-calls\">{s['tool_calls']}</td><td class=\"col-view\"><a href=\"session_{i+1}.html\">open</a></td></tr>'''\n\n    return f'''<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n<head><meta charset=\"UTF-8\"><meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1.0\"><title>Session Logs</title><style>{CSS}</style></head>\n<body><div class=\"scanline\"></div>\n<div class=\"container\">\n\n<div class=\"top-bar\">\n<div class=\"top-bar-left\"><div class=\"top-bar-logo\">{LOGO}</div><span class=\"top-bar-title\">Session Logs</span><span class=\"top-bar-badge\">v1.0</span></div>\n<div class=\"top-bar-right\"><span class=\"top-bar-dot\"></span> live &middot; {datetime.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M')}</div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"stats-grid\">\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{stats['total_sessions']}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Sessions</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{stats['total_messages']}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Messages</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{stats['total_llm_calls']}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">LLM Calls</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{stats['total_tool_calls']}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Tool Calls</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{len(stats['providers'])}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Providers</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{len(stats['models'])}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Models</div></div>\n</div>\n\n<div class=\"content-grid\">\n<div class=\"content-main\">\n\n<div class=\"term\">\n<div class=\"term-bar\"><span class=\"term-dot r\"></span><span class=\"term-dot y\"></span><span class=\"term-dot g\"></span><span class=\"term-title\">sessions</span><span>{len(sessions)} total</span></div>\n<div class=\"term-body\" style=\"padding:0\">\n<table Now let me test if the script generates correctly. But first, I realized the `!!` syntax for JS embedding will cause issues with Python's f-string formatting. The `{!!day_labels!!}` inside f-string will be interpreted as Python expressions. I need to use `{{` and `}}` to escape the braces, or use string concatenation. 

Wait, actually I need to look at this more carefully. In the f-string, `{!!day_labels!!}` would try to evaluate `!!day_labels!!` as Python code. I need to double the braces: `{{!!day_labels!!}}` would produce `{!!day_labels!!}` in the output.

But there are also weird interactions. Let me check what `!!` does in Python f-strings - `!!` is not a valid operator. So `{!!day_labels!!}` would cause a syntax error.

I need to escape all `{` in JS code as `{{` in the f-string. Let me look at all the places where `{` appears in JS:

1. `{!!day_labels!!}` -> `{{!!day_labels!!}}`
2. `{!!day_messages!!}` -> `{{!!day_messages!!}}`
3. etc.

But wait, I used `!!` as a delimiter to make it visually distinct from f-string braces. In an f-string, `{!!day_labels!!}` is indeed invalid. Let me fix by escaping.

Actually, let me think about this differently. I can use a different approach - instead of embedding `!!` markers, I should just use f-string replacement properly.

Actually the cleanest approach is to build the JS string in Python using variables, then insert it as a single f-string replacement.

Wait, there's a problem. In my `chart_js` f-string, I'm using `{{` and `}}` to escape literal braces for JS, but I'm also using actual f-string replacements like `{day_labels}`. When I have `{{` in the string, that becomes a single `{` in the output. When I have `}}` that becomes `}`. So `{{type:"bar"}}` would produce `{type:"bar"}` which is correct. But I have deeply nested structures.

Hmm, actually looking at my code more carefully, the `chart_js` f-string uses `{{` for all JS braces. But wait, I used f-string with regular single-brace references for the data variables: `var dl={day_labels},dm={day_messages},ds={day_sessions};` - these will be replaced with the JSON strings. Good.

But for the Chart.js options, I used `{{type:"bar"}}` etc. This should work: `{{` in f-string outputs `{`, `}}` outputs `}`. So this should be fine.

Wait, let me re-examine. Actually looking at my edit again:

```python
chart_js=f'''
Chart.defaults.color="#6A7894"; Chart.defaults.font.family="'Inter',sans-serif";
var dl={day_labels},dm={day_messages},ds={day_sessions};
new Chart(document.getElementById("chartDaily"),{{type:"bar",data:{{labels:dl,datasets:[{{label:"messages",data:dm,backgroundColor:"#00D4FF66",borderColor:"#00D4FF",borderWidth:1,yAxisID:"y"}},{{label:"sessions",data:ds,type:"line",borderColor:"#00FF88",backgroundColor:"#00FF8833",pointStyle:"circle",pointRadius:3,yAxisID:"y1"}}]}},options:{{responsive:true,maintainAspectRatio:false,plugins:{{legend:{{display:false}}}},scales:{{x:{{display:true,grid:{{color:"#1A1F2B"}},ticks:{{font:{{size:10}}}}}},y:{{display:false,grid:{{color:"#1A1F2B"}},beginAtZero:true}},y1:{{display:false,position:"right",grid:{{drawOnChartArea:false}},beginAtZero:true}}}}}});
...
'''
```

Let me verify this produces valid JS. In an f-string:
- `{day_labels}` is replaced with the value of `day_labels`
- `{{` becomes `{`
- `}}` becomes `}`

So `new Chart(document.getElementById("chartDaily"),{{type:"bar"...` becomes `new Chart(document.getElementById("chartDaily"),{type:"bar"...` - correct!

Let me test it.

Actually wait, I also have a potential issue. The `get_chart_data` function's model_tokens query with `COALESCE(json_extract(model,'$.id'),model,'?')` uses single quotes inside a double-quoted string. Let me check if that's fine in SQLite. Yes, SQLite accepts both `'` and `"` for strings, and since the Python string is double-quoted, the single quotes inside are fine.

Let me also check the `tools_daily` and `top_sessions` keys that aren't being used. The `tools_daily` key is set to `{}` but never populated, and `top_sessions` is queried but not rendered. I already removed ts_labels/ts_tokens from the template. That's fine - I can add a 4th chart later if needed.

The `days_sorted` items are from `chart_data['days'].items()`, which has values as lists like `[c, 0, 0]` but one of them only has 2 elements. Let me check the data structure.

The issue is in `get_chart_data` where I initialize `data['days'][d]=[c,0,0]` but then in some cases I do `data['days'][d]=[0,c,0]` or `data['days'][d]=[0,0,c]` - these all have 3 elements. But what about days that appear only in sessions or tools but not messages? 

Wait, looking at the code:
1. First loop: for messages per day, sets `data['days'][d]=[c,0,0]`
2. Second loop: for sessions per day, checks `if d in data['days']` then sets `data['days'][d][1]=c` else `data['days'][d]=[0,c,0]`
3. Third loop: for tools per day, checks if d in data['days'] then sets `data['days'][d][2]=c` else `data['days'][d]=[0,0,c]`

All ensure 3 elements. So why the error?

Let me check the chart_data more carefully. Maybe the issue is that the items are tuples and I'm unpacking wrong.

`chart_data['days'].items()` returns `(key, value)` pairs where value is a list of 3 ints. `d,_,_` should work for the key and the first 2 elements of the list...

Wait, no. `for d,_,_ in days_sorted` - `days_sorted` is `sorted(chart_data['days'].items())` which is `[(key, [a,b,c]), ...]`. So `d` = key, `_` = [a,b,c], and then we try to unpack `[a,b,c]` as `_` and there's no third element in the tuple.

Right! The items() returns (key, value) tuples, so it's 2 elements, not 3. I need to unpack as `d, v` and then unpack `v` separately.

Let me fix this.
editcompletedtmp/opencode_analytics_v7.py▶

  "filePath": "/tmp/opencode_analytics_v7.py",
  "newString": "    days_sorted=sorted(chart_data['days'].items())\n    day_labels=json.dumps([d for d,v in days_sorted])\n    day_messages=json.dumps([v[0] for d,v in days_sorted])\n    day_sessions=json.dumps([v[1] for d,v in days_sorted])",
  "oldString": "    days_sorted=sorted(chart_data['days'].items())\n    day_labels=json.dumps([d for d,_,_ in days_sorted])\n    day_messages=json.dumps([m for _,m,_ in days_sorted])\n    day_sessions=json.dumps([s for _,_,s in days_sorted])"
}

Interesting - the daily chart shows 13 days, and the token chart shows only 4 models (only those with tokens_input > 0). The sessions numbers seem off for the daily chart - looking at the data, the sessions are coming from the DB's session table (95 total across 13 days in the DB) while our log-parsed sessions are 73. That's because some sessions in the DB don't have corresponding log files. That's fine - the chart shows DB data which is the source of truth for time series.

The model tokens only shows 4 models because the query filters `WHERE tokens_input>0`. Models like `north-mini-code-free` might not have token data in their sessions.

Wait, looking more carefully at the daily chart data - the session counts show `27` on 2026-06-04 and `28` on 2026-06-06, but we only parsed 73 sessions from logs. The DB has 95 sessions total with some on dates not matching our log-parsed sessions. That's expected.

But there's a discrepancy - `2026-06-11: msgs=23 sessions=0` - that means there are messages with no session? That's odd. Actually it might be because the session was created on a different day than the messages. The session table and message table might use slightly different timing.

Everything looks good. The site is served. Let me also check the page looks right by fetching a session page to make sure it wasn't broken.

Wait, let me re-examine. Let me think about the chart config structure:

```javascript
new Chart(element, {
  type: "bar",           // level 1
  data: {                // level 2
    labels: [],
    datasets: [          // level 3 (array)
      { ... }            // level 4
    ]                    // back to level 3
  },                     // close data: back to level 2
  options: {             // level 2
    responsive: true,    // level 3
    plugins: {
      legend: { display: false }  // level 5/6
    },
    scales: {
      x: { display: true, grid: { ... }, ticks: { font: { size: 10 } } },
      y: { display: false, grid: { ... }, beginAtZero: true },
      y1: { display: false, position: "right", grid: { ... }, beginAtZero: true }
    }                    // close scales: level 3
  }                      // close options: level 2
})                       // close chart config + Chart()
```

So after `beginAtZero: true` for y1:
1. `}` close y1
2. `}` close scales
3. `}` close options
4. `}` close chart config (the 2nd argument to Chart())
5. `)` close Chart()

That's 4 `}` + `)`.

So for the daily chart I need `}}}});` after `beginAtZero:true` in the output.

In the f-string: `}}}}}}}})`  (8 `}` = 4 pairs of `}}` producing 4 `}`).

A
Assistant
deepseek-v4-flash-free | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
▶ Reasoning
I see the issue now! The `.stats-grid` has 6 columns, but there's no responsive breakpoint for it. On screens smaller than ~900px, 6 stat cards in one row will overflow or shrink to unreadable sizes. But more importantly, the stat cards and chart terms both use the `gap` property from their grid containers, and `.term + .term { margin-top:18px; }` could mess things up because inside grids, the adjacent sibling selector would still match.

Wait, inside a grid container, the `.term + .term` selector won't match because `.term` elements are grid children but not siblings of each other in the parent grid. Each `.term` is a direct child of the grid container. But the `+` selector would only match if they're consecutive siblings. In a grid, they ARE consecutive siblings. So `.term + .term` would add `margin-top:18px` between terms in a grid.

In the `.charts-row` grid, the 3 chart terms are consecutive siblings: `term.chart-term + term.chart-term`. So `.term + .term { margin-top:18px }` would add 18px between them. But the grid already has `gap:12px`. So the spacing would be 18px + 12px = 30px. That's not 0 though.

In the `.side-panel` flex container, the 3 terms are also consecutive siblings. Same issue — double spacing.

In the session page, the 4 terms are consecutive siblings. They'd get `margin-top:18px` between them, which is the desired behavior.

But in the `.charts-row`, the double spacing (gap + margin) is wrong. I should only apply the margin-top when the term is NOT inside a grid/flex container.

The cleanest fix is to only apply `margin-top` to `.term` elements that are NOT in a grid/flex context. But CSS can't detect that. 

Alternative approach: Instead of `.term + .term`, just wrap the session page terms in a div with a gap.

Let me do that. It's the cleanest approach.
editcompletedtmp/opencode_analytics_v7.py▶
Input
{
  "filePath": "/tmp/opencode_analytics_v7.py",
  "newString": ".term {\n  background:#131720; border:1px solid #2A3040; border-radius:10px;\n  overflow:hidden;\n}",
  "oldString": ".term {\n  background:#131720; border:1px solid #2A3040; border-radius:10px;\n  overflow:hidden;\n}\n.term + .term { margin-top:18px; }"
}

Fixed the spacing issues:

- **Removed** `margin-bottom: 18px` from `.term` (was conflicting with grid/flex gaps)
- **Added** `.term-stack` wrapper for session pages with `display:flex; gap:18px` so terms stack with consistent spacing there
- **Added** `margin-bottom: 18px` to `.stats-grid` and `.charts-row` so sections have proper gaps between them
- **Added** responsive breakpoints for `.stats-grid` (6→3→2 columns) and `.content-grid` (collapses to 1 column) so cards don't get squished at smaller viewports

```css
.top-bar-logo {
  font-family:'JetBrains Mono',monospace; font-size:0.6em; line-height:1.2;

}
```

The `line-height:1.2` with `font-size:0.6em` means each line is 0.72em. With 5 original lines that's 3.6em. With 8 lines that's 5.76em. It'll be taller but might still work with the flex centering.

  "description": "Replace LOGO with figlet output"

{
  "description": "Find secrets on server",
  "prompt": "Search this server for secrets that might be exposed in opencode conversation logs. Look for:\n\n1. Gemini API keys - search for patterns like `AIzaSy` (gemini key prefix) in config files, environment, and the opencode DB\n2. SIP credentials - search for sip credentials in config files\n3. OpenAI/Codex login credentials - search for openai credentials, API keys starting with `sk-`, login tokens\n4. Any other API keys, tokens, passwords, or secrets\n\nSearch these locations:\n- `/root/.local/share/opencode/` - opencode config, DB, and logs\n- Environment variables (`env`, `export`)\n- Common config files: `~/.bashrc`, `~/.config/`, `/etc/`\n- The opencode SQLite DB: `/root/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db`\n\nUse `rg` (ripgrep) with patterns like:\n- `AIzaSy` (Gemini API key prefix)\n- `sk-` (OpenAI API key prefix)\n- `sip:` or `sip.` \n- `password`, `api_key`, `apikey`, `secret`, `token`\n- `gemini`\n- `openai`\n- Login credentials (username:password patterns)\n\nFor the SQLite DB, use `sqlite3` to query:\n- `SELECT data FROM message WHERE data LIKE '%AIzaSy%'`\n- `SELECT data FROM part WHERE data LIKE '%AIzaSy%'`\n- `SELECT data FROM message WHERE data LIKE '%sk-%'`\n- `SELECT data FROM part WHERE data LIKE '%sk-%'`\n- `SELECT data FROM message WHERE data LIKE '%sip:%'`\n- `SELECT data FROM part WHERE data LIKE '%sip:%'`\n- etc.\n\nAlso check:\n- /root/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db for the credential table\n- Any .env files\n- /etc/environment\n\nReturn ALL secrets found, their locations, and the exact patterns/values. Be very thorough - check the DB messages and parts for API keys, tokens, credentials in the conversation text itself.",
  "subagent_type": "explore"

8. **Postgres Password**: `[REDACTED]`
9. **Pretix Django Secret**: `[REDACTED]`
10. **OpenAI Client ID**: `[REDACTED]`

- Gemini API key: `[REDACTED]`
- OpenAI refresh token: starts with `rt.1.`
- OpenAI JWT: starts with `eyJ` (all JWTs start with eyJ)
- SIP passwords: specific hex strings
- Postgres password: [REDACTED] hex string
- Server password: [REDACTED]
- OpenAI client ID: `[REDACTED]`

For safety, I should also use generic patterns:
- Any `sk-...` (OpenAI API key format)
- Any `AIza...` (Google API key format)
- Any JWT tokens (strings starting with `eyJ` that are long enough)
- Any `rt.1.` (refresh tokens)
- Email addresses (PII)
- Any `sip:` URIs with credentials

Let me add the redaction function right after the LOGO definition and before parse_log.
editcompletedtmp/opencode_analytics_v7.py▶
Input
{
  "filePath": "/tmp/opencode_analytics_v7.py",
  "newString": "  __/ |                                             \n |___/                                              '''\n\n# \u2500\u2500 Secrets redaction \u2500\u2500\nSECRETS = [\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n    '[REDACTED]',\n]\nSECRET_PATTERNS = [\n    (re.compile(r'eyJ[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+\\.[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+\\.[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+'), 'eyJ...'),\n    (re.compile(r'[Aa][Pp][Ii]_?[Kk][Ee][Yy].*?=[\\'\\\"][^\\'\\\"]+'), 'API_KEY=REDACTED'),\n]\n\ndef redact(text):\n    for s in SECRETS:\n        if s in text:\n            text = text.replace(s, '[REDACTED]')\n    for pat, sub in SECRET_PATTERNS:\n        text = pat.sub(sub, text)\n    return text\n\ndef redact_html(text):\n    return html.escape(redact(text))\n\ndef redact_obj(obj):\n    \"\"\"Recursively redact strings in a JSON-serializable object.\"\"\"\n    if isinstance(obj, str):\n        return redact(obj)\n    elif isinstance(obj, dict):\n        return {k: redact_obj(v) for k, v in obj.items()}\n    elif isinstance(obj, list):\n        return [redact_obj(v) for v in obj]\n    return obj",
  "oldString": "  __/ |                                             \n |___/                                              '''"
}
Output exit=

{
  "filePath": "/tmp/opencode_analytics_v7.py",
  "newString": "def render_chat_html(messages):\n    if not messages: return '<p style=\"color:#6A7894\">No messages found.</p>'\n    parts=[]\n    for msg in messages:\n        if msg['role']=='user':\n            text='\\n'.join(p.get('text','') for p in msg['parts'] if p.get('type')=='text')\n            parts.append(f'''<div class=\"chat-msg chat-user\"><div class=\"chat-avatar\">U</div><div class=\"chat-bubble\"><div class=\"chat-role\">User</div><div class=\"chat-text\">{redact_html(text)}</div></div></div>''')\n        elif msg['role']=='assistant':\n            finish=msg['data'].get('finish',''); model_id=msg['data'].get('modelID','') or msg['data'].get('model',''); agent=msg['data'].get('agent','')\n            tch=\"\"; tr=\"\"; rr=\"\"\n            for p in msg['parts']:\n                t=p.get('type','')\n                if t=='text': tr=p.get('text','')\n                elif t=='reasoning': rr=p.get('text','')\n                elif t=='tool':\n                    nm=p.get('tool','?'); st=p.get('state',{}); status=st.get('status','')\n                    inp=st.get('input',{}); outp=st.get('output',''); meta=st.get('metadata',{}); title=st.get('title','')\n                    tid=f\"t_{msg['id']}_{nm}_{hash(json.dumps(inp))&0xFFFFFF}\"\n                    inp_str=json.dumps(redact_obj(inp),indent=2) if isinstance(inp,dict) else redact(str(inp))\n                    outp_str=redact(str(outp))[:500]+('...' if len(str(outp))>500 else '')\n                    meta_info=''.join(f' exit={meta.get(\"exit\",\"\")}' if meta.get(\"exit\")!='' else '' for _ in[0])+(' truncated' if meta.get('truncated') else '')\n                    tch+=f'''<div class=\"tool-call\"><div class=\"tool-header\" onclick=\"tt('{tid}')\"><span class=\"tool-name\">{html.escape(nm)}</span><span class=\"tool-status\">{html.escape(status)}</span><span class=\"tool-title\">{html.escape(title)}</span><span class=\"tool-toggle\">&#9654;</span></div><div class=\"tool-body\" id=\"{tid}\" style=\"display:none\"><div class=\"tool-section\"><div class=\"tool-section-title\">Input</div><pre>{html.escape(inp_str)}</pre></div><div class=\"tool-section\"><div class=\"tool-section-title\">Output{meta_info}</div><pre>{html.escape(outp_str)}</pre></div></div></div>'''\n                elif t=='file':\n                    tch+=f'''<div class=\"tool-call\"><div class=\"tool-header\"><span class=\"tool-name\">file</span><span class=\"tool-title\">{html.escape(p.get(\"filename\",\"?\"))}</span></div></div>'''\n            hp=[x for x in([html.escape(model_id)]if model_id else[])+([f\"agent={html.escape(agent)}\"]if agent else[])+([f\"finish={html.escape(finish)}\"]if finish else[])]\n            hs=' | '.join(hp)\n            rh=f'''<div class=\"reasoning\" onclick=\"tt('r_{msg['id']}')\">&#9654; Reasoning</div><div class=\"reasoning-body\" id=\"r_{msg['id']}\"><pre>{redact_html(rr)}</pre></div>''' if rr else ''\n            th=f'<div class=\"chat-text\">{redact_html(tr)}</div>' if tr else ''\n            parts.append(f'''<div class=\"chat-msg chat-assistant\"><div class=\"chat-avatar\">A</div><div class=\"chat-bubble\"><div class=\"chat-role\">Assistant</div><div class=\"chat-meta\">{hs}</div>{rh}{th}{tch}</div></div>''')\n    return '\\n'.join(parts) if parts else '<p style=\"color:#6A7894\">No messages.</p>'",
  "oldString": "def render_chat_html(messages):\n    if not messages: return '<p style=\"color:#6A7894\">No messages found.</p>'\n    parts=[]\n    for msg in messages:\n        if msg['role']=='user':\n            text='\\n'.join(p.get('text','') for p in msg['parts'] if p.get('type')=='text')\n            parts.append(f'''<div class=\"chat-msg chat-user\"><div class=\"chat-avatar\">U</div><div class=\"chat-bubble\"><div class=\"chat-role\">User</div><div class=\"chat-text\">{html.escape(text)}</div></div></div>''')\n        elif msg['role']=='assistant':\n            finish=msg['data'].get('finish',''); model_id=msg['data'].get('modelID','') or msg['data'].get('model',''); agent=msg['data'].get('agent','')\n            tch=\"\"; tr=\"\"; rr=\"\"\n            for p in msg['parts']:\n                t=p.get('type','')\n                if t=='text': tr=p.get('text','')\n                elif t=='reasoning': rr=p.get('text','')\n                elif t=='tool':\n                    nm=p.get('tool','?'); st=p.get('state',{}); status=st.get('status','')\n                    inp=st.get('input',{}); outp=st.get('output',''); meta=st.get('metadata',{}); title=st.get('title','')\n                    tid=f\"t_{msg['id']}_{nm}_{hash(json.dumps(inp))&0xFFFFFF}\"\n                    inp_str=json.dumps(inp,indent=2) if isinstance(inp,dict) else str(inp)\n                    outp_str=str(outp)[:500]+('...' if len(str(outp))>500 else '')\n                    meta_info=''.join(f' exit={meta.get(\"exit\",\"\")}' if meta.get(\"exit\")!='' else '' for _ in[0])+(' truncated' if meta.get('truncated') else '')\n                    tch+=f'''<div class=\"tool-call\"><div class=\"tool-header\" onclick=\"tt('{tid}')\"><span class=\"tool-name\">{html.escape(nm)}</span><span class=\"tool-status\">{html.escape(status)}</span><span class=\"tool-title\">{html.escape(title)}</span><span class=\"tool-toggle\">&#9654;</span></div><div class=\"tool-body\" id=\"{tid}\" style=\"display:none\"><div class=\"tool-section\"><div class=\"tool-section-title\">Input</div><pre>{html.escape(inp_str)}</pre></div><div class=\"tool-section\"><div class=\"tool-section-title\">Output{meta_info}</div><pre>{html.escape(outp_str)}</pre></div></div></div>'''\n                elif t=='file':\n                    tch+=f'''<div class=\"tool-call\"><div class=\"tool-header\"><span class=\"tool-name\">file</span><span class=\"tool-title\">{html.escape(p.get(\"filename\",\"?\"))}</span></div></div>'''\n            hp=[x for x in([html.escape(model_id)]if model_id else[])+([f\"agent={html.escape(agent)}\"]if agent else[])+([f\"finish={html.escape(finish)}\"]if finish else[])]\n            hs=' | '.join(hp)\n            rh=f'''<div class=\"reasoning\" onclick=\"tt('r_{msg['id']}')\">&#9654; Reasoning</div><div class=\"reasoning-body\" id=\"r_{msg['id']}\"><pre>{html.escape(rr)}</pre></div>''' if rr else ''\n            th=f'<div class=\"chat-text\">{html.escape(tr)}</div>' if tr else ''\n            parts.append(f'''<div class=\"chat-msg chat-assistant\"><div class=\"chat-avatar\">A</div><div class=\"chat-bubble\"><div class=\"chat-role\">Assistant</div><div class=\"chat-meta\">{hs}</div>{rh}{th}{tch}</div></div>''')\n    return '\\n'.join(parts) if parts else '<p style=\"color:#6A7894\">No messages.</p>'"
}

The user just asked "how is the site structure" - a simple description.
Three file types, all self-contained static HTML (no JS dependencies beyond Chart.js CDN):

```
/var/www/analytics.gulasch.site/
├── index.html              (47K)  — dashboard with stats, 3 charts, session table, side panel
├── analytics-report.json   (23K)  — full data dump
└── session_{1..74}.html    (14K–1.6M each) — one per session, full chat + tool calls + timeline
```

**`index.html`** — top bar (figlet logo + title), 6 stat cards, 3 Chart.js charts (daily activity, hourly distribution, token usage), two-column layout: session table (left, 74 rows) + side panel (providers, models with bars, tools leaderboard), status bar.

**`session_N.html`** — top bar, stacked terminal windows: info cards (provider/model/msg/tool counts), tools used, full conversation (user/assistant messages with collapsible reasoning & tool calls), timeline (last 30 log events), back link, status bar. Sizes vary with conversation length — the smallest is 14K (1 message), the largest is 1.6MB (longest session).

A
Assistant
deepseek-v4-flash-free | agent=build | finish=stop
▶ Reasoning
The user asked about how sessions are represented in opencode. Let me explain the data model based on what I've seen from the DB schema and log format.
A session in opencode is a single AI-assisted task run. It has three levels:

**`session`** — the top-level container:
- `id` (`ses_xxx`) — unique identifier
- `slug` (`warm-thunder`) — human-readable name
- `project_id`, `directory`, `path` — what project it ran in
- `agent` (`build`/`explore`/`general`) — which agent type
- `model` — JSON blob `{"id":"gpt-5.5","providerID":"openai","variant":"high"}`
- `cost`, `tokens_input/output/reasoning/cache_*` — usage tracking
- `time_created/updated` — timestamps

**`message`** — one turn in the conversation, belongs to a session:
- `id` — unique
- `session_id` — FK to session
- `data` — JSON with `role` (`user`/`assistant`), `modelID`, `providerID`, `finish` reason

**`part`** — a content chunk within a message:
- `message_id` — FK to message
- `data` — JSON with `type` (`text`/`tool`/`reasoning`/`file`), plus type-specific fields like `tool`, `state` (with `input`/`output`/`status`)

The hierarchy is: **session → messages → parts**. A session typically has multiple messages (user asks, assistant responds), and each assistant message can contain text, reasoning blocks, and tool calls with their inputs/outputs.

{
  "command": "python3 -c \"\nimport sqlite3, json\nconn = sqlite3.connect('/root/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db')\ncur = conn.cursor()\n\n# Per-model: sessions, messages, tokens, tools used\ncur.execute(\\\"\\\"\\\"SELECT json_extract(data,'$.modelID'), COUNT(DISTINCT session_id), COUNT(*), \n  SUM(tokens_input), SUM(tokens_output), SUM(tokens_reasoning)\n  FROM message WHERE json_extract(data,'$.role')='assistant' \n  AND json_extract(data,'$.modelID') IS NOT NULL\n  AND tokens_input>0\n  GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC\\\"\\\"\\\")\nprint('Model stats:')\nfor r in cur.fetchall():\n    print(f'  {r[0]:30s} sessions={r[1]} msgs={r[2]} in={r[3]} out={r[4]} reas={r[5]}')\n\n# Per-tool: sessions, calls, models that use it\ncur.execute(\\\"\\\"\\\"SELECT json_extract(data,'$.tool'), COUNT(DISTINCT session_id), COUNT(*)\n  FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool'\n  AND json_extract(data,'$.tool') IS NOT NULL\n  GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC\\\"\\\"\\\")\nprint()\nprint('Tool stats:')\nfor r in cur.fetchall():\n    print(f'  {r[0]:25s} sessions={r[1]} calls={r[2]}')\n\n# Tool co-occurrence (which tools are used together in same session)\ncur.execute(\\\"\\\"\\\"SELECT a.tool, b.tool, COUNT(*) FROM\n  (SELECT session_id, json_extract(data,'$.tool') as tool FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool') a\n  JOIN (SELECT session_id, json_extract(data,'$.tool') as tool FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool') b\n  ON a.session_id=b.session_id AND a.tool < b.tool\n  GROUP BY a.tool, b.tool ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 20\\\"\\\"\\\")\nprint()\nprint('Tool co-occurrence (top 20 pairs):')\nfor r in cur.fetchall():\n    print(f'  {r[0]:15s} + {r[1]:15s} = {r[2]}')\n\n# Sessions per model for time series\ncur.execute(\\\"\\\"\\\"SELECT json_extract(data,'$.modelID'), date(m.time_created/1000,'unixepoch'), COUNT(*)\n  FROM message m WHERE json_extract(data,'$.role')='assistant'\n  AND json_extract(data,'$.modelID') IS NOT NULL\n  GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 1,2\\\"\\\"\\\")\nprint()\nprint('Messages per model per day:')\nfor r in cur.fetchall():\n    print(f'  {r[0]:25s} {r[1]} = {r[2]}')\n\"",
  "description": "Explore model/tool data for pages"
}

  "command": "python3 -c \"\nimport sqlite3, json\nconn = sqlite3.connect('/root/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db')\ncur = conn.cursor()\n\n# Per-model: sessions, messages, token stats (from session table)\ncur.execute(\\\"\\\"\\\"SELECT COALESCE(json_extract(s.model,'$.id'),s.model,'?'),\n  COUNT(DISTINCT s.id), COUNT(DISTINCT m.id),\n  SUM(s.tokens_input), SUM(s.tokens_output), SUM(s.tokens_reasoning),\n  SUM(s.tokens_cache_read), SUM(s.cost)\n  FROM session s LEFT JOIN message m ON m.session_id=s.id\n  WHERE s.tokens_input>0\n  GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY SUM(s.tokens_input) DESC\\\"\\\"\\\")\nprint('Model stats (from session table):')\nfor r in cur.fetchall():\n    print(f'  {r[0]:30s} sessions={r[1]} msgs={r[2]} in={r[3]:,} out={r[4]:,} reas={r[5]:,} cache_read={r[6]:,} cost={r[7]}')\n\n# Per-tool: sessions, calls, messages containing tool calls\ncur.execute(\\\"\\\"\\\"SELECT json_extract(data,'$.tool'), COUNT(DISTINCT session_id), COUNT(*),\n  COUNT(DISTINCT message_id)\n  FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool'\n  AND json_extract(data,'$.tool') IS NOT NULL\n  GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC\\\"\\\"\\\")\nprint()\nprint('Tool stats:')\nfor r in cur.fetchall():\n    print(f'  {r[0]:25s} sessions={r[1]} calls={r[2]} msgs={r[3]}')\n\n# Tool co-occurrence\ncur.execute(\\\"\\\"\\\"SELECT a.tool, b.tool, COUNT(*) FROM\n  (SELECT session_id, json_extract(data,'$.tool') as tool FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool') a\n  JOIN (SELECT session_id, json_extract(data,'$.tool') as tool FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool') b\n  ON a.session_id=b.session_id AND a.tool < b.tool\n  GROUP BY a.tool, b.tool ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 20\\\"\\\"\\\")\nprint()\nprint('Tool co-occurrence (top 20):')\nfor r in cur.fetchall():\n    print(f'  {r[0]:15s} + {r[1]:15s} = {r[2]}')\n\"",
  "description": "Get model and tool data"
}

deepseek-v4-flash-free | agent=build | finish=tool-calls
▶ Reasoning
The tool co-occurrence numbers are inflated because they're joining on session_id across all tool calls in the session. A session might have 100 bash calls and 50 read calls, so the pair (bash, read) would count 100 * 50 = 5000 times. The co-occurrence should be counted by unique sessions, not by total calls.

Let me redesign the approach and implement it properly. Let me plan:

1. **Model pages** (`model_gpt-5.5.html`, `model_deepseek-v4-flash-free.html`, etc.):
   - Model name, provider
   - Total sessions, messages, tokens (input/output/reasoning/cache)
   - Sessions using this model (table)
   - Token usage over time (chart)
   - Tool usage with this model
   
2. **Tool pages** (`tool_bash.html`, `tool_read.html`, etc.):
   - Tool name
   - Total calls, sessions, messages containing it
   - Sessions using this tool (table)
   - Models that use this tool
   - Tool calls over time (chart)
   - Sessions that also used related tools

3. **Clustering**:
   - By model: which sessions used which models
   - By agent type
   - By tool usage patterns (tools that are often used together)
   - Session duration clusters
   - Something visual like a tool co-occurrence matrix

  "newString": "</div>\n<script>{chart_js}</script>\n</body></html>'''\n\ndef gen_nav_link(label, href):\n    return f'<a href=\"{href}\" style=\"display:inline-flex;align-items:center;gap:6px;padding:6px 14px;border:1px solid #2A3040;border-radius:6px;color:#6A7894;text-decoration:none;font-size:0.78em;font-family:JetBrains Mono;transition:all 0.2s;\" onmouseover=\"this.style.borderColor=\\'#00D4FF\\';this.style.color=\\'#00D4FF\\'\" onmouseout=\"this.style.borderColor=\\'#2A3040\\';this.style.color=\\'#6A7894\\'\">{label}</a>'\n\ndef gen_model_pages(stats, sessions):\n    conn=sqlite3.connect(str(DB_PATH)); cur=conn.cursor()\n    cur.execute(\"\"\"SELECT COALESCE(json_extract(s.model,'$.id'),s.model,'?'),\n      json_extract(s.model,'$.providerID'), COUNT(DISTINCT s.id), SUM(s.tokens_input), SUM(s.tokens_output),\n      SUM(s.tokens_reasoning), SUM(s.tokens_cache_read)\n      FROM session s WHERE s.tokens_input>0 GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY SUM(s.tokens_input) DESC\"\"\")\n    models_db = {r[0]:{'provider':r[1],'sessions':r[2],'tokens_in':r[3],'tokens_out':r[4],'tokens_reas':r[5],'tokens_cache':r[6]} for r in cur.fetchall()}\n    cur.execute(\"\"\"SELECT COALESCE(json_extract(s.model,'$.id'),s.model,'?'), s.id, s.slug, s.tokens_input+s.tokens_output+s.tokens_reasoning\n      FROM session s WHERE s.tokens_input>0 AND s.slug IS NOT NULL ORDER BY 1, 3 DESC\"\"\")\n    model_sessions = {}\n    for r in cur.fetchall():\n        model_sessions.setdefault(r[0], []).append({'id':r[1],'slug':r[2],'tokens':r[3]})\n    cur.execute(\"\"\"SELECT COALESCE(json_extract(s.model,'$.id'),s.model,'?'), json_extract(p.data,'$.tool'), COUNT(*)\n      FROM session s JOIN part p ON p.session_id=s.id WHERE s.tokens_input>0 AND json_extract(p.data,'$.type')='tool' AND json_extract(p.data,'$.tool') IS NOT NULL\n      GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 1,3 DESC\"\"\")\n    model_tools = {}\n    for r in cur.fetchall():\n        model_tools.setdefault(r[0], []).append((r[1], r[2]))\n    conn.close()\n    for model_name, m in models_db.items():\n        safe = re.sub(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]', '_', model_name)\n        ms = model_sessions.get(model_name, [])\n        mt = model_tools.get(model_name, [])\n        tool_rows = '\\n'.join(\n            f'<div class=\"tool-rank\"><span class=\"tool-rank-name\">{html.escape(t)}</span><span class=\"tool-rank-count\">{c}</span></div>'\n            for t,c in mt[:10])\n        sess_rows = '\\n'.join(\n            f'<tr><td class=\"col-name\">{html.escape(s[\"slug\"][:30])}</td><td class=\"col-tokens\">{s[\"tokens\"]:,}</td></tr>'\n            for s in ms[:20])\n        html_page = f'''<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\"><head><meta charset=\"UTF-8\"><meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1.0\"><title>Model: {html.escape(model_name)}</title><style>{CSS}\n.hero {{ display:flex; gap:24px; align-items:center; margin-bottom:18px; }}\n.hero-icon {{ width:48px; height:48px; border-radius:10px; background:#00D4FF22; border:1px solid #00D4FF44; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; font-size:1.4em; font-weight:700; color:#00D4FF; font-family:JetBrains Mono; }}\n.hero-info h1 {{ font-size:1.3em; color:#C5CDD9; margin:0; }}\n.hero-info .sub {{ font-size:0.85em; color:#6A7894; }}\n.stat-big {{ display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr); gap:12px; margin-bottom:18px; }}\n</style></head>\n<body><div class=\"scanline\"></div>\n<div class=\"container\">\n<div class=\"top-bar\"><div class=\"top-bar-left\"><div class=\"top-bar-logo\">{LOGO}</div><span class=\"top-bar-title\">model: {html.escape(model_name)}</span></div>\n<div class=\"top-bar-right\">{gen_nav_link('&larr; index', 'index.html')}</div></div>\n<div class=\"hero\">\n<div class=\"hero-icon\">M</div>\n<div class=\"hero-info\"><h1>{html.escape(model_name)}</h1><div class=\"sub\">{html.escape(m[\"provider\"] or \"?\")} &middot; {m[\"sessions\"]} sessions &middot; {m[\"tokens_in\"]+m[\"tokens_out\"]+m[\"tokens_reas\"]:,} total tokens</div></div>\n</div>\n<div class=\"stat-big\">\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{m[\"sessions\"]}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Sessions</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{m[\"tokens_in\"]+m[\"tokens_out\"]+m[\"tokens_reas\"]:,}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Tokens</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{m[\"tokens_in\"]:,}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Input</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{m[\"tokens_out\"]:,}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Output</div></div>\n</div>\n<div class=\"content-grid\">\n<div class=\"content-main\">\n<div class=\"term\"><div class=\"term-bar\"><span class=\"term-dot r\"></span><span class=\"term-dot y\"></span><span class=\"term-dot g\"></span><span class=\"term-title\">sessions ({len(ms)})</span></div>\n<div class=\"term-body\" style=\"padding:0\"><table class=\"sessions-table\"><thead><tr><th>Session</th><th>Tokens</th></tr></thead><tbody>{sess_rows}</tbody></table></div></div>\n</div>\n<div class=\"side-panel\">\n<div class=\"term\"><div class=\"term-bar\"><span class=\"term-dot r\"></span><span class=\"term-dot y\"></span><span class=\"term-dot g\"></span><span class=\"term-title\">tools used with this model</span></div>\n<div class=\"term-body\">{tool_rows}</div></div>\n</div></div>\n<div class=\"status-bar\"><div class=\"status-left\"><span class=\"status-item\"><span class=\"status-dot cyan\"></span> {m[\"sessions\"]} sessions</span></div>\n<div class=\"status-right\"><span class=\"status-item\">generated {datetime.now().strftime('%H:%M')}</span></div></div>\n</div></body></html>'''\n        with open(OUTPUT_DIR/f\"model_{safe}.html\",'w') as f: f.write(html_page)\n    print(f\"  Generated {len(models_db)} model pages\")\n\ndef gen_tool_pages(stats, sessions):\n    conn=sqlite3.connect(str(DB_PATH)); cur=conn.cursor()\n    cur.execute(\"\"\"SELECT json_extract(data,'$.tool'), COUNT(DISTINCT session_id), COUNT(*), COUNT(DISTINCT message_id)\n      FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool' AND json_extract(data,'$.tool') IS NOT NULL\n      GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC\"\"\")\n    tools_db = {}\n    for r in cur.fetchall():\n        tools_db[r[0]] = {'sessions':r[1],'calls':r[2],'messages':r[3]}\n    cur.execute(\"\"\"SELECT json_extract(data,'$.tool'), session_id, COUNT(*)\n      FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool' AND json_extract(data,'$.tool') IS NOT NULL\n      GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 1,3 DESC\"\"\")\n    tool_sessions = {}\n    for r in cur.fetchall():\n        tool_sessions.setdefault(r[0], []).append({'id':r[1],'calls':r[2]})\n    cur.execute(\"\"\"SELECT a.tool, b.tool FROM\n      (SELECT DISTINCT session_id, json_extract(data,'$.tool') as tool FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool') a\n      JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT session_id, json_extract(data,'$.tool') as tool FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool') b\n      ON a.session_id=b.session_id AND a.tool < b.tool\"\"\")\n    cooc = {}\n    for r in cur.fetchall():\n        k = (r[0], r[1])\n        cooc[k] = cooc.get(k, 0) + 1\n    conn.close()\n    count = 0\n    for tool_name, t in tools_db.items():\n        safe = re.sub(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]', '_', tool_name)\n        ts = tool_sessions.get(tool_name, [])\n        related = sorted([(b,c) for (a,b),c in cooc.items() if a==tool_name] + [(a,c) for (a,b),c in cooc.items() if b==tool_name], key=lambda x:-x[1])[:10]\n        rel_rows = '\\n'.join(\n            f'<div class=\"tool-rank\"><span class=\"tool-rank-name\">{html.escape(r[0])}</span><span class=\"tool-rank-count\">{r[1]} sessions</span></div>'\n            for r in related)\n        sess_rows = '\\n'.join(\n            f'<tr><td class=\"col-name\">{html.escape(s[\"id\"][:25])}</td><td class=\"col-calls\">{s[\"calls\"]}</td></tr>'\n            for s in ts[:20])\n        html_page = f'''<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\"><head><meta charset=\"UTF-8\"><meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1.0\"><title>Tool: {html.escape(tool_name)}</title><style>{CSS}\n.hero {{ display:flex; gap:24px; align-items:center; margin-bottom:18px; }}\n.hero-icon {{ width:48px; height:48px; border-radius:10px; background:#FF8A5022; border:1px solid #FF8A5044; display:flex; align-items:center; justify-content:center; font-size:1.4em; font-weight:700; color:#FF8A50; font-family:JetBrains Mono; }}\n.hero-info h1 {{ font-size:1.3em; color:#C5CDD9; margin:0; }}\n.hero-info .sub {{ font-size:0.85em; color:#6A7894; }}\n.stat-big {{ display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(4,1fr); gap:12px; margin-bottom:18px; }}\n</style></head>\n<body><div class=\"scanline\"></div>\n<div class=\"container\">\n<div class=\"top-bar\"><div class=\"top-bar-left\"><div class=\"top-bar-logo\">{LOGO}</div><span class=\"top-bar-title\">tool: {html.escape(tool_name)}</span></div>\n<div class=\"top-bar-right\">{gen_nav_link('&larr; index', 'index.html')}</div></div>\n<div class=\"hero\">\n<div class=\"hero-icon\">T</div>\n<div class=\"hero-info\"><h1>{html.escape(tool_name)}</h1><div class=\"sub\">{t[\"calls\"]} calls &middot; {t[\"messages\"]} messages &middot; {t[\"sessions\"]} sessions</div></div>\n</div>\n<div class=\"stat-big\">\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{t[\"calls\"]}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Total Calls</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{t[\"sessions\"]}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Sessions</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{t[\"messages\"]}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Messages</div></div>\n<div class=\"stat-card\"><div class=\"stat-value\">{round(t[\"calls\"]/max(t[\"sessions\"],1),1)}</div><div class=\"stat-label\">Calls/Session</div></div>\n</div>\n<div class=\"content-grid\">\n<div class=\"content-main\">\n<div class=\"term\"><div class=\"term-bar\"><span class=\"term-dot r\"></span><span class=\"term-dot y\"></span><span class=\"term-dot g\"></span><span class=\"term-title\">sessions using {html.escape(tool_name)} ({len(ts)})</span></div>\n<div class=\"term-body\" style=\"padding:0\"><table class=\"sessions-table\"><thead><tr><th>Session ID</th><th>Calls</th></tr></thead><tbody>{sess_rows}</tbody></table></div></div>\n</div>\n<div class=\"side-panel\">\n<div class=\"term\"><div class=\"term-bar\"><span class=\"term-dot r\"></span><span class=\"term-dot y\"></span><span class=\"term-dot g\"></span><span class=\"term-title\">related tools</span></div>\n<div class=\"term-body\">{rel_rows or '<div style=\"color:#6A7894;font-size:0.85em\">No related tools</div>'}</div></div>\n</div></div>\n<div class=\"status-bar\"><div class=\"status-left\"><span class=\"status-item\"><span class=\"status-dot orange\"></span> {t[\"calls\"]} calls</span></div>\n<div class=\"status-right\"><span class=\"status-item\">generated {datetime.now().strftime('%H:%M')}</span></div></div>\n</div></body></html>'''\n        with open(OUTPUT_DIR/f\"tool_{safe}.html\",'w') as f: f.write(html_page)\n        count += 1\n    print(f\"  Generated {count} tool pages\")\n\ndef gen_cluster_page(stats, sessions):\n    conn=sqlite3.connect(str(DB_PATH)); cur=conn.cursor()\n    cur.execute(\"\"\"SELECT a.tool, b.tool, COUNT(DISTINCT a.session_id)\n      FROM (SELECT DISTINCT session_id, json_extract(data,'$.tool') as tool FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool') a\n      JOIN (SELECT DISTINCT session_id, json_extract(data,'$.tool') as tool FROM part WHERE json_extract(data,'$.type')='tool') b\n      ON a.session_id=b.session_id AND a.tool < b.tool\n      GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 3 DESC LIMIT 30\"\"\")\n    cooc = [(r[0],r[1],r[2]) for r in cur.fetchall()]\n    cooc_rows = '\\n'.join(\n        f'<tr><td class=\"col-name\">{html.escape(a)}</td><td class=\"col-name\">{html.escape(b)}</td><td class=\"col-calls\">{c}</td></tr>'\n        for a,b,c in cooc)\n    tools_list = sorted(set(a for a,b,c in cooc) | set(b for a,b,c in cooc))\n    tool_idx = {t:i for i,t in enumerate(tools_list)}\n    n = len(tools_list)\n    matrix = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]\n    for a,b,c in cooc:\n        i,j = tool_idx[a], tool_idx[b]\n        matrix[i][j] = matrix[j][i] = c\n    matrix_css = '\\n'.join(\n        f'.mc{i} {{ background:rgba(0,212,255,{min(v/30,1):.2f}); }}'\n        for i,row in enumerate(matrix) for j,v in enumerate(row) if v and j>i)\n    matrix_html = '<table class=\"cooc-table\"><tr><th></th>'+''.join(f'<th class=\"cooc-h\">{html.escape(t[:6])}</th>' for t in tools_list)+'</tr>'\n    for i,t in enumerate(tools_list):\n        matrix_html += f'<tr><td class=\"cooc-label\">{html.escape(t[:8])}</td>'\n        for j in range(n):\n            v = matrix[i][j]\n            if v:\n                matrix_html += f'<td class=\"cooc-cell mc{i}\" title=\"{tools_list[j]}: {v}\">{v}</td>'\n            else:\n                matrix_html += '<td class=\"cooc-cell\"></td>'\n        matrix_html += '</tr>'\n    agent_counts = {}\n    for s in sessions:\n        a = s.get('agent','?') or '?'\n        agent_counts[a] = agent_counts.get(a,0)+1\n    agent_rows = '\\n'.join(\n        f'<div class=\"tool-rank\"><span class=\"tool-rank-name\">{html.escape(a)}</span><span class=\"tool-rank-count\">{c} sessions</span></div>'\n        for a,c in sorted(agent_counts.items(), key=lambda x:-x[1]))\n    html_page = f'''<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\"><head><meta charset=\"UTF-8\"><meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width,initial-scale=1.0\"><title>Patterns & Clusters</title><style>{CSS}\n.cooc-table {{ border-collapse:collapse; font-size:0.72em; font-family:JetBrains Mono; }}\n.cooc-table td, .cooc-table th {{ padding:3px 6px; border:1px solid #2A3040; text-align:center; min-width:20px; }}\n.cooc-table .cooc-h {{ writing-mode:vertical-lr; text-orientation:mixed; height:60px; font-weight:400; color:#6A7894; }}\n.cooc-table .cooc-label {{ text-align:right; color:#6A7894; font-weight:500; }}\n.cooc-cell {{ color:#C5CDD9; }}\n{matrix_css}\n.insight-card {{ background:#1A1F2B; border:1px solid #2A3040; border-radius:8px; padding:16px; margin-bottom:12px; }}\n.insight-card .ic-title {{ font-weight:600; font-size:0.85em; color:#C5CDD9; margin-bottom:4px; }}\n.insight-card .ic-body {{ font-size:0.82em; color:#6A7894; line-height:1.6; }}\n</style></head>\n<body><div class=\"scanline\"></div>\n<div class=\"container\">\n<div class=\"top-bar\"><div class=\"top-bar-left\"><div class=\"top-bar-logo\">{LOGO}</div><span class=\"top-bar-title\">patterns &amp; clusters</span></div>\n<div class=\"top-bar-right\">{gen_nav_link('&larr; index', 'index.html')}</div></div>\n<div class=\"content-grid\">\n<div class=\"content-main\">\n<div class=\"term\"><div class=\"term-bar\"><span class=\"term-dot r\"></span><span class=\"term-dot y\"></span><span class=\"term-dot g\"></span><span class=\"term-title\">tool co-occurrence matrix</span><span>{len(tools_list)} tools</span></div>\n<div class=\"term-body\" style=\"overflow-x:auto\">{matrix_html}</div></div>\n<div class=\"term\"><div class=\"term-bar\"><span class=\"term-dot r\"></span><span class=\"term-dot y\"></span><span class=\"term-dot g\"></span><span class=\"term-title\">top co-occurring pairs</span></div>\n<div class=\"term-body\" style=\"padding:0\"><table class=\"sessions-table\"><thead><tr><th>Tool A</th><th>Tool B</th><th>Sessions</th></tr></thead><tbody>{cooc_rows}</tbody></table></div></div>\n</div>\n<div class=\"side-panel\">\n<div class=\"term\"><div class=\"term-bar\"><span class=\"term-dot r\"></span><span class=\"term-dot y\"></span><span class=\"term-dot g\"></span><span class=\"term-title\">sessions by agent</span></div>\n<div class=\"term-body\">{agent_rows}</div></div>\n<div class=\"insight-card\"><div class=\"ic-title\">Key Insights</div>\n<div class=\"ic-body\">\u2022 {len(tools_list)} tools form a co-occurrence network<br>\n\u2022 Most sessions use 2-4 tools together<br>\n\u2022 bash + read co-occur in {cooc[0][2] if cooc else 0} sessions<br>\n\u2022 Primary agent: {max(agent_counts, key=agent_counts.get) if agent_counts else '?'}<br>\n\u2022 {stats[\"total_sessions\"]} total sessions analyzed</div></div>\n</div></div>\n<div class=\"status-bar\"><div class=\"status-left\"><span class=\"status-item\"><span class=\"status-dot purple\"></span> {len(tools_list)} tools in matrix</span></div>\n<div class=\"status-right\"><span class=\"status-item\">generated {datetime.now().strftime('%H:%M')}</span></div></div>\n</div></body></html>'''\n    with open(OUTPUT_DIR/\"patterns.html\",'w') as f: f.write(html_page)\n    print(\"  Generated patterns page\")",
  "oldString": "</div>\n<script>{chart_js}</script>\n</body></html>'''"
}

Wait, the model extraction logic in the gen_session_page call is fragile. The `model` variable in the session page template is `html.escape(session['model'] or 'Unknown')`. The model field in sessions can be a JSON string like `{"id":"gpt-5.5","providerID":"openai"}` or just a model name. Let me fix this by extracting the model name properly.

Actually, looking at `gen_session_page`, the model variable is:
```python
model=html.escape(session['model'] or 'Unknown')
```

taskcompletedExplore server activities▶
Input
{
  "description": "Explore server activities",
  "subagent_type": "explore",
  "prompt": "Very thoroughly explore what this server is and what happened on it. This is to build a complete picture of all activities for a server analytics page. \n\nSearch these areas:\n\n1. **System info**: `uname -a`, `uptime`, `cat /etc/os-release`, `df -h`, `free -h`, `lscpu`\n\n2. **Running processes**: `ps aux --sort=-%mem | head -40`, `systemctl list-units --type=service --state=running`, `docker ps -a` (if docker exists), what ports are listening (`ss -tlnp` or `netstat -tlnp`)\n\n3. **Web servers / domains**: Check `/etc/caddy/Caddyfile` or `/etc/nginx/` for all hosted sites/domains, check `/var/www/` for what's being served\n\n4. **Cron jobs**: `crontab -l`, `ls -la /etc/cron*`, check systemd timers\n\n5. **All projects and configs**: \n   - List all dirs in `/opt/`, `/srv/`, `/var/www/`\n   - Check for Docker Compose files (`find /opt -name \"docker-compose*\" -o -name \"compose.*\" 2>/dev/null`)\n   - Check for .env files (`find /opt /root -name \".env\" -not -path \"*/node_modules/*\" 2>/dev/null`)\n   - Check opencode configs (`/root/.config/opencode/`, `~/.local/share/opencode/`)\n\n6. **Logs from various services**:\n   - `/var/log/` - system logs\n   - `/root/.local/share/opencode/log/` - opencode logs\n   - `/opt/*/log/` if any\n   - Journalctl: `journalctl --list-boots`\n\n7. **All users**: `cat /etc/passwd | grep -E ':/home|:/root'`, check `~/.ssh/` for SSH keys\n\n8. **Recent file changes**: `find /opt /root /etc -name \"*.py\" -newer /tmp -type f 2>/dev/null | head -30`\n\n9. **Git repos**: `find /opt /root -name \".git\" -type d -not -path \"*/node_modules/*\" 2>/dev/null`\n\n10. **History**: Check `~/.bash_history` for recent commands, especially things like apt, docker, git, etc.\n\n11. **Any monitoring or analytics tools installed**: `which prometheus grafana netdata cockpit htop btop glances` etc.\n\nReturn a comprehensive summary of:\n- What this server is (purpose, hostname, specs)\n- All services running (web apps, databases, tools)\n- All domains hosted\n- What people were doing with opencode on this server (building projects, managing infrastructure)\n- Timeline of key events\n- Interesting facts"
}

The user wants the analytics page to cover everything that happened on the server, not just opencode sessions. Let me now prepare an overview for them - a summary of what I found and what I can add to the analytics site.

Let me think about what data I should add to the analytics site:

1. **Server overview** - specs, uptime, OS
2. **All domains hosted** - gulasch.site and all subdomains with their purposes
3. **All services** - all systemd services running
4. **Timeline of events** - when things were built
5. **Opencode sessions stats** - what we already have
6. **Projects built** - the key projects
7. **System stats** - disk, memory, CPU

I should propose to the user what to add. Let me give them a concise overview and ask what they'd like to see on the analytics page.
Here's what I found — this server (`ubuntu-16gb-fsn1-1`, Hetzner, 8 vCPU, 16GB RAM, 300GB SSD) is a **personal homelab** running **37 systemd services** across ~20 domains. It's one person's (Zebreus) infrastructure for the German n50.lat hacker community.

**Domains hosted** (all via Caddy on 178.105.108.45):
| Category | Domains |
|---|---|
| Core | `gulasch.site` (opencode UI), `analytics.gulasch.site` |
| Event | `n50.camp`, `wiki.n50.camp`, `tickets.n50.camp` → tickets.n50.lat |
| Web apps | `pretickets.gulasch.site` (Pretix ticketing), `terminal.gulasch.site` (Ghostty), `drive.gulasch.site` |
| AI projects | `aisaas.gulasch.site` (ScoutBot), `bagger.gulasch.site` (relationship AI game), `conversation.gulasch.site`, `richard.gulasch.site` (SIP bots) |
| Dev | `c3cock.gulasch.site` (pet habitat editor), `angular.gulasch.site`, `wiki.gulasch.site`, `3d.gulasch.site`, various test/deploy sites |

**What opencode built** (74 sessions): n50.camp website (CSS 3D masterpiece), C3COCK shared editor, both SIP bots (Richard rickrolls callers, Conversation has real Gemini voice chat), bagger game, SignalScout AI, Pretix deployment, Gulasch Drive, both wikis, the Ghostty web terminal, and the analytics site itself.

**Key timeline**: Server provisioned ~June 4. Two intense phases — June 4-5 (initial projects) and June 11-14 (n50.camp marathon, 17 backup iterations in 10 hours). Currently 2h uptime after a reboot.

I can expand the analytics site to include a **server dashboard** showing: system stats (disk/mem/CPU), all domains with status, all services, a project timeline, and the full opencode analytics already built. Want me to build this as a new main page with the current analytics as a subsection?

2. Session slugs (they sometimes hint at the project)
3. The log samples we already captured
4. Key files mentioned (like Caddyfile, docker-compose, etc.)

Let me query the DB for this information.
taskcompletedMap sessions to projects▶
Input
{
  "description": "Map sessions to projects",
  "subagent_type": "explore",
  "prompt": "Map each opencode session to a project. The opencode database is at `/root/.local/share/opencode/opencode.db`.\n\nI need to determine which project each session worked on by analyzing the tool calls (bash commands, reads, edits, writes) in each session.\n\nThe projects on this server are:\n- **n50.camp** - the main event website (Astro/CSS 3D tent scene). Files likely in `/srv/n50.camp/`\n- **C3COCK** - pet habitat configurator, editor at `c3cock.gulasch.site`. Files in `/opt/c3cock/`\n- **aisaas / SignalScout** - B2B AI, in `/opt/aisaas/`  \n- **bagger** - relationship AI game, in `/opt/bagger/` or `/opt/bagger-api/`\n- **pretix** - ticketing system, in `/opt/pretickets.gulasch.site/`\n- **wiki** - MediaWiki instances, likely configs in `/etc/nginx/` and `/var/www/`\n- **opencode config** - the opencode setup itself\n- **SIP bots** (conversation-sip, rickroll-sip) - in `/opt/conversation-sip/` and `/opt/rickroll-sip/`\n- **gulasch-drive** - leaderboard, in `/opt/gulasch-drive/`\n- **ghostty-web** - web terminal, in `/opt/ghostty-web-server/`\n- **analytics** - the analytics dashboard itself\n- **gulasch-sites-repo** - Nix flake deployment, in `/srv/gulasch-sites-repo/`\n\nFor each session ID, look at:\n1. The bash commands in the part table - search for file paths, directory operations, project names\n2. The session slug (is it in the DB session table?)\n3. The messages content for project references\n\nQuery approaches:\n```sql\n-- Get session IDs and their tool calls with bash commands\nSELECT p.session_id, json_extract(p.data, '$.tool'), json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') \nFROM part p \nWHERE json_extract(p.data, '$.type')='tool' \n  AND json_extract(p.data, '$.tool')='bash'\n  AND json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') IS NOT NULL\nLIMIT 50;\n\n-- Or get session_id and check for project keywords in bash commands\nSELECT p.session_id, substr(json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command'), 1, 200)\nFROM part p \nWHERE json_extract(p.data, '$.type')='tool' \n  AND json_extract(p.data, '$.tool')='bash'\n  AND (\n    json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%n50%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%c3cock%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%aisaas%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%bagger%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%pretix%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%wiki%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%sip%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%drive%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%ghostty%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%analytics%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%opencode%'\n    OR json_extract(p.data, '$.state.input.command') LIKE '%gulasch%'\n  )\nLIMIT 200;\n```\n\nAlso check the session table itself:\n```sql\nSELECT slug, model, substr(directory,1,60), agent, time_created FROM session WHERE slug IS NOT NULL ORDER BY time_created;\n```\n\nAnd check the opencode log files for project hints:\n```bash\n# Check log files for project names mentioned\nrg -l \"n50|c3cock|aisaas|bagger|pretix|drive|ghostty|sip|wiki|analytics|gulasch\" /root/.local/share/opencode/log/ --no-filename | sort -u\n```\n\nReturn a mapping of session_ids (or first ~30 chars) to project names. Try to match every session to at least a broad category like \"infrastructure\", \"n50.camp\", \"web app\", \"game\", \"analytics\", \"SIP bot\", \"wiki\", \"configuration\" etc. Some sessions may be mixed (working on multiple projects) - note those."
}

'cosmic-eagle':'infrastructure','eager-island':'infrastructure',\n    'misty-mountain':'opencode','swift-sailor':'opencode','neon-circuit':'opencode',\n    'proud-panda':'yamd',\n}\n\ndef
